content:
Character: Dorian Valey
Perception: Dorian is Aldo's grandson — same jawline, same watchful eyes, none of the old man's ferocious drive. Handsome kid, well-read, haunted by something he can't name. He keeps my rent where Aldo set it, which I appreciate, and he actually reads the articles I slide under his door, which I appreciate more. We've had good conversations on the rooftop — he once asked me whether I was afraid my life's work might be wrong, and I told him the only wasted life is an uncommitted one. He went quiet after that. I think he heard it as an indictment. He sees me as an eccentric tenant. I see him as a young man drowning in options. He needs a megalodon of his own.
summary:
Character: Dorian Valey
Perception: Dorian is Aldo's grandson — same jawline, same watchful eyes, none of the old man's ferocious drive. Handsome kid, well-read, haunted by something he can't name. He keeps my rent where Aldo set it, which I appreciate, and he actually reads the articles I slide under his door, which I appreciate more. We've had good conversations on the rooftop — he once asked me whether I was afraid my life's work might be wrong, and I told him the only wasted life is an uncommitted one. He went quiet after that. I think he heard it as an indictment. He sees me as an eccentric tenant. I see him as a young man drowning in options. He needs a megalodon of his own.
